sources.notes: Statement and prefix maximum invariance constraints.
Official editorial confirms maintaining contributions under prefix-maximum constraints.
Luogu solution used to cross-check the monotonic stack/segment tree viewpoints.

annotation.summary: Answer whether transformations preserve prefix maximum information under updates/queries.
annotation.constraints: The input size requires logarithmic or linearithmic preprocessing and query handling.
annotation.core_idea: A position contributes only when a suitable previous prefix maximum threshold exists. Rephrase the condition as maintaining valid contribution intervals or nearest dominating values, then use a segment tree or monotonic stack plus binary search to test each case efficiently.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) or O(n) preprocessing plus efficient queries depending on implementation
tricks: Convert prefix maximum invariance into local contribution conditions.
Use nearest greater/smaller structure or segment tree queries to find the blocking threshold.
Alternative sparse-table/binary-search implementations answer the same range condition.

solution_variants.name: contribution-threshold-query
solution_variants.summary: Maintain the contribution condition with a segment tree over values/ranges and locate the first threshold that can change a prefix maximum.

solution_variants.name: monotonic-stack-boundaries
solution_variants.summary: Precompute nearest structural boundaries with a monotonic stack and answer the same feasibility condition with range queries.

tags.evidence: The main reduction is deciding which positions still contribute to the prefix maximum structure.
A common implementation keeps range contribution/threshold information in a segment tree.
An alternative solution precomputes nearest relevant boundaries with a monotonic stack.
Binary search can locate the first position/value where a prefix maximum condition changes.
Sparse table range maximum queries are another way to check the precomputed boundaries.
